verdict.answer_first: A states the answer immediately: "Yes — the second comparison is the real problem"; B starts softer with "A couple of things worth flagging" before answering.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to a cleaned-up recommendation and adds the needed caveat: "if (userInput == null || String(userInput).trim() === '')"; B surveys alternatives and ends by handing off: "Want me to adjust based on the expected type?"
verdict.fabrication: B makes an unsupported production-frequency claim: "The one that bites people in production is #1"; A frames unknowns as conditional: "depending on context I can't see".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to apply because it gives the safer fix plus a control-flow warning: "make it return reject(...)"; B's second option can reintroduce rejecting non-strings after warning about that risk.
